syllabi.title: Construction and Materials Syllabus - Terminale F4 (Building Construction / Civil Engineering)
syllabi.subject: Construction and Materials

extracted_metadata.subject: Construction and Materials
competencies: Analyze historical and contemporary developments in construction technology
Evaluate building assemblies and structural systems holistically
Integrate sustainable construction strategies and resource-efficient materials
Collaborate across architectural and engineering disciplines for system design

modules.title: Development of Construction Technologies
modules.description: Examines the historical drivers and obstacles of innovation in building technology from the 20th century to the present day.
objectives: Identify drivers of innovation such as industrialization, materials science, and computational technology
Understand historical obstacles, including industry fragmentation and specialized design consultancy
Assess contemporary global market demands and technological transfer in materials and process engineering

modules.title: Statics of Architectural Structures
modules.description: Introduces basic structural morphology, equilibrium conditions, and load-transfer mechanisms of architectural components.
objectives: Understand structural morphology and basic structural elements
Formulate and solve equilibrium equations for basic force systems
Analyze material behavior under architectural dead and live loads

modules.title: Elements of Building and Building Systems
modules.description: Explores the systematic organization, performance requirements, lifetimes, cost implications, and standard specifications of core building assemblies.
objectives: Define and classify building systems: foundations, superstructure, exterior envelope, interior partitions, and mechanical systems
Analyze the lifetime, durability, and cost over time for each primary building system
Apply Construction Specifications Institute (CSI) Division placements to building materials and systems

modules.title: Sustainable Strategies and Systems Integration
modules.description: Analyzes building energy usage, resource efficiency, and the synergistic integration of architectural systems based on standard performance mandates.
objectives: Evaluate international and domestic energy consumption trends related to buildings
Identify and implement resource-efficient strategies, low-e materials, and end-of-life materials reuse
Integrate the six performance mandates: spatial, thermal, air quality, acoustical, visual, and building integrity

assessmentCriteria: Ability to analyze structural statics and material behavior in architectural contexts
Proper classification and specification of materials based on CSI division standards
Integration of structural, envelope, and environmental control systems in design assignments
Inclusion of sustainable construction strategies and life-cycle costing in final solutions
extracted_metadata.curriculumStructure: The curriculum is structured into three progressive components: introducing core architectural principles and technical evolution, teaching structural mechanics and materials behavior, and analyzing the engineering, performance, and specification of integrated building systems.
gradingStandards: Familiarity with functional and performance requirements of architectural assemblies
Demonstration of a collaborative approach to solving multi-disciplinary construction situations
Quality, logic, and completeness in practical design/build exercises
Creativity and engineering rigor in building systems integration
learningOutcomes: Familiarity with functional and technical requirements of complex architectural assemblies
Clear understanding of the construction process and the design-to-build workflow
Ability to identify opportunities for engineering and material invention within standard codes
Development of practical collaboration strategies between construction and design disciplines

syllabi.title: Construction and Materials Syllabus - Upper Sixth (Civil Engineering)
syllabi.subject: Construction and Materials

extracted_metadata.subject: Construction and Materials
competencies: Analyze the cultural, historical, and technical evolution of building structures
Apply structural mechanics, including statics, equilibrium equations, and material behavior, to design building systems
Integrate core building subsystems into holistic, sustainable, and resource-efficient architectural assemblies
Apply Construction Specifications Institute (CSI) specifications to define and catalog building elements

modules.title: Principles and Scope of Building Technology
modules.description: Exploration of the cultural context of technology, holistic design principles, statics of architectural structures, and sustainable resource strategies.
objectives: Explain how contemporary technology derives from historical and cultural evolution
Formulate basic equilibrium equations and evaluate material behavior under force systems
Identify best practice sustainable strategies and resource-efficient processes in architectural structures

modules.title: Development and Innovation in Construction Technologies
modules.description: A study of historical and modern drivers for innovation in construction, encompassing materials science, computational technologies, and the barriers to industry-wide development.
objectives: Analyze the drivers of innovation such as industrialization, modularization, and materials science
Contrast technological successes and failures in construction between 1900 and 1980
Evaluate contemporary barriers to innovation, including fragmentation of design processes and low R&D investment

modules.title: Elements of Building Systems and Specifications
modules.description: Detailed examination of the primary physical components of building construction and their classification according to professional specification codes.
objectives: Define the six core building systems: Foundation, Superstructure, Exterior Envelope, Interior Partitions, Mechanical Systems, and Furnishings
Compare the expected lifespans and relative cost percentages of primary building components over time
Map building systems to their respective Construction Specifications Institute (CSI) Division specifications

assessmentCriteria: Accuracy in applying structural equilibrium equations and analyzing material behaviors
Integration level of building subsystems (Site, Structure, Skin, Space Plan, Services, Stuff) in technical drawings and design schemes
Correct assignment of Construction Specifications Institute (CSI) Division codes to specified materials
Inclusion of sustainable, resource-efficient strategies in student project solutions
extracted_metadata.curriculumStructure: The curriculum is structured into three parts: Course Principles & Scope, Historical and Modern Technological Developments, and Building Elements & Subsystems Integrated with Professional Specifications.
gradingStandards: Evaluation of student-designed integrated building system projects
Assessment of practical problem-solving in architectural statics and structural morphology
Grading of written coursework and technical documentation accuracy
learningOutcomes: Familiarity with the technical and performance requirements of architectural assemblies
Understanding of a broad range of standard and advanced design solutions
Ability to analyze contemporary issues and design-construction workflows
Capability to coordinate across architectural, structural, and mechanical disciplines
